it.","marks":[],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"This article is based on real ReportPortal launch data from 2021–2025, so you can benchmark your that sends results, logs, metadata, and structure into the platform.","marks":[],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"High usage of an agent usually is community knowledge, examples, and fewer "edge case" surprises ","marks":[],"data":{}}]}]}]},{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"In other words, agent usage is a practical proxy for ecosystem maturity and integration risk.","marks":[],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"heading-2","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"Visibility across test frameworks","marks":[],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"Many large projects today use multiple test frameworks at the same time. UI tests, API tests, integration checks, and legacy suites often live in different stacks – each producing its own result format, logs, and reports.","marks":[],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"Without centralized reporting, teams quickly end up:","marks":[],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"unordered-list","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"list-item","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"Jumping between HTML reports, CI logs, and raw artifacts ","marks":[],"data":{}}]}]},{"nodeType":"list-item","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"Manually correlating failures across pipelines ","marks":[],"data":{}}]}]},{"nodeType":"list-item","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"Losing historical context once runs disappear from CI storage ","marks":[],"data":{}}]}]}]},{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"ReportPortal solves this by collecting test results from different frameworks, languages, and pipelines into a ","marks":[],"data":{}},{"nodeType":"hyperlink","data":{"uri":"https://reportportal.io/blog/all-your-test-results-one-place-smarter-reporting-across-tools"},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"single reporting tool","marks":[{"type":"underline"}],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"text","value":".","marks":[],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"That’s what makes framework distribution analysis possible in the first place – and why it reflects real production usage instead of isolated experiments.","marks":[],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"Whether your UI tests run in Playwright, your API tests in Pytest, or your suites in JUnit, all results land in one place:","marks":[],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"unordered-list","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"list-item","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"One dashboard ","marks":[],"data":{}}]}]},{"nodeType":"list-item","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"One data model ","marks":[],"data":{}}]}]},{"nodeType":"list-item","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"One historical view of quality","marks":[],"data":{}}]}]}]},{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"This is why reporting volume is not just a metric – it’s a signal of what teams actually trust to run in production.","marks":[],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"heading-2","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"How test framework usage has changed","marks":[],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"Based on % of ReportPortal launches","marks":[{"type":"italic"}],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"embedded-asset-block","data":{"target":{"sys":{"id":"BZg7IwKoMkzmYS27Axwno","type":"Link","linkType":"Asset"}}},"content":[]},{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"Looking at five years of launch data, three clear trends stand out: ","marks":[],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"ordered-list","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"list-item","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"JavaScript frameworks are now dominant ","marks":[],"data":{}}]}]},{"nodeType":"list-item","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"Java remains stable but no longer leading ","marks":[],"data":{}}]}]},{"nodeType":"list-item","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"Reporting volume is consolidating around fewer tools ","marks":[],"data":{}}]}]}]},{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"The growth of JavaScript testing frameworks in ReportPortal also aligns with broader ecosystem signals – including rising npm download volumes – suggesting that these trends reflect real industry adoption.","marks":[],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"The most notable shift is the rapid rise of Playwright, which moved from a secondary position in 2023 (16.2%) to the clear #1 framework in 2024–2025, reaching 29% of all launches in 2025.","marks":[],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"Cypress, despite periodic "end-of-life" discussions in the community, continues to show stable usage across many existing automation suites.","marks":[],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"At the same time, former leaders like TestNG and Cucumber steadily declined.","marks":[],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"heading-2","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"How testing ecosystems differ in practice","marks":[],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"Behind the framework names are different testing cultures, infrastructure choices, and reporting needs.","marks":[],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"table","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"table-row","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"table-header-cell","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"Ecosystem","marks":[{"type":"bold"}],"data":{}}]}]},{"nodeType":"table-header-cell","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"What the data shows","marks":[{"type":"bold"}],"data":{}},{"nodeType":"text","value":" ","marks":[],"data":{}}]}]},{"nodeType":"table-header-cell","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"What this ","marks":[],"data":{}}]}]},{"nodeType":"table-header-cell","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"Benchmark check","marks":[{"type":"bold"}],"data":{}},{"nodeType":"text","value":" ","marks":[],"data":{}}]}]},{"nodeType":"table-cell","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"~65–70% of ReportPortal launches by 2025. The dominant ecosystem. ","marks":[],"data":{}}]}]},{"nodeType":"table-cell","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"Used for UI, API, and component testing with strong CI/CD integration and mature reporting practices. ","marks":[],"data":{}}]}]},{"nodeType":"table-cell","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"If you run Playwright or WebdriverIO without centralized reporting, you are behind many similar teams. ","marks":[],"data":{}}]}]}]},{"nodeType":"table-row","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"table-cell","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"Java","marks":[{"type":"bold"}],"data":{}},{"nodeType":"text","value":" ","marks":[],"data":{}}]}]},{"nodeType":"table-cell","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"Still generates significant reporting volume, but its share is gradually shrinking. ","marks":[],"data":{}}]}]},{"nodeType":"table-cell","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"Remains common for large regression suites and enterprise systems with long-lived test infrastructure. ","marks":[],"data":{}}]}]},{"nodeType":"table-cell","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"If your project relies on static HTML reports or CI logs, you miss analytics many teams already use. ","marks":[],"data":{}}]}]}]},{"nodeType":"table-row","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"table-cell","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"Python","marks":[{"type":"bold"}],"data":{}},{"nodeType":"text","value":" ","marks":[],"data":{}}]}]},{"nodeType":"table-cell","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"Smaller share, but usage remains stable over time. ","marks":[],"data":{}}]}]},{"nodeType":"table-cell","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"Popular for API, data-driven testing, and fast feedback workflows. ","marks":[],"data":{}}]}]},{"nodeType":"table-cell","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"If Pytest results exist only as CI artifacts, you lose long-term insight into failures and trends. ","marks":[],"data":{}}]}]}]},{"nodeType":"table-row","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"table-cell","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":".NET","marks":[{"type":"bold"}],"data":{}},{"nodeType":"text","value":" ","marks":[],"data":{}}]}]},{"nodeType":"table-cell","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"Small but steady presence (NUnit, xUnit, related frameworks). ","marks":[],"data":{}}]}]},{"nodeType":"table-cell","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"Typically used in long-running enterprise test suites requiring cross-project visibility. ","marks":[],"data":{}}]}]},{"nodeType":"table-cell","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"If results stay in CI logs or local reports, you miss centralized insights into failures and trends. ","marks":[],"data":{}}]}]}]}]},{"nodeType":"heading-2","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"From insight to action","marks":[],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"Framework distribution by reporting volume is not only a popularity ranking.
It reflects real usage and real strategic direction.","marks":[],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"Here is how teams use this data in practice.","marks":[],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"1. Plan framework migrations before they become urgent","marks":[{"type":"bold"}],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"When a framework steadily loses reporting volume year over year, it rarely happens by accident.","marks":[],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"Teams use this signal to: ","marks":[],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"unordered-list","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"list-item","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"Identify frameworks that are quietly becoming legacy ","marks":[],"data":{}}]}]},{"nodeType":"list-item","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"Audit critical test suites still running on them ","marks":[],"data":{}}]}]},{"nodeType":"list-item","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"Plan migrations while expertise and community support still exist ","marks":[],"data":{}}]}]}]},{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"This turns migration discussions into concrete, evidence-based decisions:
"This framework is down to 5% of our volume and has been shrinking for three years – let’s plan to move off it next year".","marks":[],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"2. Align skills, hiring, and training with real usage","marks":[{"type":"bold"}],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"Year-by-year framework distribution also acts as a skills map for the organization.","marks":[],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"Teams use it to:","marks":[],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"unordered-list","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"list-item","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"Focus internal training on frameworks that are clearly gaining share ","marks":[],"data":{}}]}]},{"nodeType":"list-item","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"Prioritize hiring profiles aligned with dominant CI tooling ","marks":[],"data":{}}]}]},{"nodeType":"list-item","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"Invest in shared libraries, templates, and best practices where adoption is growing","marks":[],"data":{}}]}]}]},{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"Instead of relying on external trend reports, teams rely on their own data:
"Our ReportPortal data shows this framework doubled its volume in two years – it should be a first-class skill in our team".","marks":[],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"3. Justify investments and evaluate experiments","marks":[{"type":"bold"}],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"Multi-year reporting data connects tooling decisions with outcomes.","marks":[],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"Teams use it to: ","marks":[],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"unordered-list","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"list-item","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"Evaluate whether framework pilots actually stuck ","marks":[],"data":{}}]}]},{"nodeType":"list-item","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"Correlate training or CI investments with real adoption ","marks":[],"data":{}}]}]},{"nodeType":"list-item","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"Identify high-cost frameworks that no longer justify ongoing support  ","marks":[],"data":{}}]}]}]},{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"In roadmap and budget discussions, this data replaces opinions with evidence.","marks":[],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"heading-2","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"Why this perspective matters","marks":[],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"Many discussions about test frameworks stay theoretical.
ReportPortal shows what teams actually connect, run, and maintain every day.","marks":[],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"That means: ","marks":[],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"unordered-list","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"list-item","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"Less guesswork ","marks":[],"data":{}}]}]},{"nodeType":"list-item","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"Lower long-term risk ","marks":[],"data":{}}]}]},{"nodeType":"list-item","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"Faster time to value once reporting is integrated  ","marks":[],"data":{}}]}]}]},{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"Framework distribution by reporting volume turns execution data into strategic insight and helps teams evolve their testing stack deliberately, not accidentally.","marks":[],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"By 2025, ReportPortal usage clearly shows a shift toward JavaScript-based frameworks, with Playwright leading by a wide margin.","marks":[],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"Java remains stable, Python stays consistent, and .NET remains niche but dependable.","marks":[],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"If your framework aligns with these trends, integration risk is low. If it doesn’t, this data gives you a realistic roadmap for what to adopt – and how to do it safely.","marks":[],"data":{}}]}]}

title.title: Test framework trends: insights from ReportPortal reporting volume

leadParagraph.leadParagraph: In 2025, nearly 77% of all ReportPortal launches came from just a handful of agents. Here’s what teams actually use to send test results into ReportPortal – and what that says about real-world test automation today.

articleBody.raw: {"nodeType":"document","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"We are pleased to announce that ReportPortal has successfully completed its annual SOC 2 Type II audit for the latest reporting period. The independent assessment confirmed that our security and operational controls were appropriately designed and operated effectively throughout the audit timeframe. For enterprise security, compliance, procurement, and vendor risk teams, this renewal provides validated assurance and helps streamline vendor evaluation processes.","marks":[],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"This marks the third consecutive year that ReportPortal has renewed its SOC 2 attestation – demonstrating that our security program is not a one-time initiative, but a sustained and mature operational discipline embedded across our organization.","marks":[],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"heading-2","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"What SOC 2 Type II means","marks":[],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"SOC 2 Type II is an independent third-party audit conducted in accordance with the AICPA Trust Services Criteria: Security, Availability, Processing Integrity, Confidentiality, and Privacy.","marks":[],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"Unlike a point-in-time certification, SOC 2 Type II evaluates whether controls operate effectively over a defined period. Auditors examine detailed evidence – including policies, access controls, monitoring activities, change management procedures, and risk management practices – to verify consistent execution.","marks":[],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"The audit requires the provision of extensive documentation and artifacts. Thanks to well-established security processes within ReportPortal and the broader governance framework of EPAM, we did not need to implement urgent remediation efforts to meet audit expectations. Instead, we demonstrated through evidence that our controls were already functioning as required.","marks":[],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"heading-2","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"Why this matters now","marks":[],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"Over the past decades, business operations have increasingly shifted online. Organizations now rely heavily on cloud-based infrastructure – including IaaS, PaaS, and SaaS models – to reduce capital expenditures and accelerate innovation. While this model increases flexibility and efficiency, it also raises expectations around data protection and operational resilience.","marks":[],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"In regulated industries such as financial services, non-compliance can lead to significant regulatory and reputational consequences. Standards like PCI DSS underscore the importance of maintaining strong safeguards for sensitive information.","marks":[],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"As businesses increasingly entrust third-party providers with confidential data, the stakes continue to rise. Organizations must ensure that sensitive data is protected from unauthorized access, misuse, or unintended exposure – including scenarios where private data could be improperly used, for example, to train large language models (LLMs).","marks":[],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"At the same time, vendor security questionnaires alone are often insufficient. Security teams cannot always independently verify vendor statements. SOC 2 addresses this challenge by providing independent validation based on auditor-reviewed evidence – not just written assurances.","marks":[],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"Today, SOC 2 has effectively become a baseline requirement for enterprise third-party risk management programs. Security maturity must extend across the entire supply chain. It is not enough for an organization to be secure internally – its vendors must meet comparable standards. Independent audits help ensure there are no weak links, because once a security incident occurs, remediation is already reactive.","marks":[],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"Our multi-year SOC 2 renewal demonstrates long-term commitment to maintaining these expectations.","marks":[],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"heading-2","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"What this means for ReportPortal customers","marks":[],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"This renewal directly supports customer needs by enabling faster vendor onboarding, easier security reviews, and increased confidence in ReportPortal’s security program.","marks":[],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"For enterprise procurement and security teams, a current SOC 2 Type II attestation reduces evaluation friction by providing independently validated assurance of control effectiveness. It simplifies due diligence processes and supports internal approval workflows.","marks":[],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"By maintaining SOC 2 compliance year after year, we help our customers move forward with greater confidence in the strength and stability of our security controls.","marks":[],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"heading-2","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"Security as an ongoing commitment","marks":[],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"Beyond supporting customer reviews and onboarding processes, SOC 2 renewal reflects a broader, long-term security commitment. SOC 2 renewal is part of a broader, continuous security strategy. Security at ReportPortal is integrated across organizational processes and supported at every level. We maintain structured risk assessments, ongoing monitoring, governance controls, and continuous improvement practices to ensure our safeguards remain effective as our platform evolves.","marks":[],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"By renewing SOC 2 for the third consecutive year, ReportPortal reinforces its commitment to transparency, accountability, and enterprise-grade security standards.","marks":[],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"heading-2","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"SOC 2 vs. SOC 3: understanding the difference","marks":[],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"To support different stages of vendor evaluation, ReportPortal provides both SOC 2 and SOC 3 reports:","marks":[],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"unordered-list","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"list-item","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"SOC 2 Type II report","marks":[{"type":"bold"}],"data":{}},{"nodeType":"text","value":"\nA detailed report that describes our control environment, testing procedures, and auditor results. Because it contains sensitive information about internal controls, it is typically shared under NDA upon request. ","marks":[],"data":{}}]}]},{"nodeType":"list-item","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"SOC 3 report\n","marks":[{"type":"bold"}],"data":{}},{"nodeType":"text","value":"A high-level summary confirming successful completion of the SOC 2 audit. It does not include detailed control descriptions and can be shared publicly without NDA.","marks":[],"data":{}}]}]}]},{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"For many organizations, the SOC 3 report provides sufficient initial assurance to proceed with evaluation, while the SOC 2 Type II report offers deeper transparency for full security reviews. When customers need assurance for periods after the SOC report end date, we can also provide ","marks":[],"data":{}},{"nodeType":"text","value":"bridge letters","marks":[{"type":"bold"}],"data":{}},{"nodeType":"text","value":" – management attestations summarizing any material changes to our control environment since the last report, helping close gaps between audit cycles during vendor risk reviews.","marks":[],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"To request the ","marks":[],"data":{}},{"nodeType":"text","value":"SOC 2 Type II report or SOC 3 report","marks":[{"type":"bold"}],"data":{}},{"nodeType":"text","value":", please reach out via our official ","marks":[],"data":{}},{"nodeType":"hyperlink","data":{"uri":"https://reportportal.io/contact-us/general"},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"contact form","marks":[{"type":"underline"}],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"text","value":".","marks":[],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"Our team is ready to support your security and compliance evaluation process.","marks":[],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"","marks":[],"data":{}}]}]}

title.title: ReportPortal renews SOC 2 Type II attestation for the third consecutive year

leadParagraph.leadParagraph: We are pleased to announce that ReportPortal has successfully completed its annual SOC 2 Type II audit for the latest reporting period.

articleBody.raw: {"nodeType":"document","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"ReportPortal is widely used for analyzing automated test results, aggregating logs, classifying failures with ML, and tracking quality trends over time. When used correctly, it significantly improves feedback speed and decision-making.","marks":[],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"However, many teams fail to unlock its full potential. The problem is rarely the tool itself – it’s how teams analyze and interpret the data.","marks":[],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"Below are the most common mistakes teams make when working with test results in ReportPortal, along with practical advice on how to avoid them.","marks":[],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"1. Analyzing reports without a clear goal","marks":[{"type":"bold"}],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"A frequent mistake is reviewing reports without a specific purpose. Teams open a dashboard, notice the number of failed tests, and move on.","marks":[],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"ReportPortal provides data, not conclusions. Without clear questions, analysis remains superficial. Unhelpful questions:","marks":[],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"unordered-list","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"list-item","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"How many tests failed?\"","marks":[],"data":{}}]}]},{"nodeType":"list-item","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"\"Is the build green?\"","marks":[],"data":{}}]}]}]},{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":" Better questions:","marks":[],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"unordered-list","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"list-item","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"Which failure types are increasing over time?","marks":[],"data":{}}]}]},{"nodeType":"list-item","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"Which tests are the flakiest?","marks":[],"data":{}}]}]},{"nodeType":"list-item","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"Where do we lose the most time during triage?","marks":[],"data":{}}]}]}]},{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"How to avoid it:\n","marks":[{"type":"bold"}],"data":{}},{"nodeType":"text","value":"Define the goal before analyzing results – release quality, test stability, feedback speed, or technical debt. Ignoring custom defect types","marks":[{"type":"bold"}],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"Many teams rely only on default ","marks":[],"data":{}},{"nodeType":"hyperlink","data":{"uri":"https://reportportal.io/docs/work-with-reports/InvestigationOfFailure#set-defect-type"},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"defect types","marks":[{"type":"underline"}],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"text","value":" (Product Bug, Automation Bug, System Issue, No Defect, To Investigate) and never go further. As a result:","marks":[],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"unordered-list","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"list-item","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"all failures look the same","marks":[],"data":{}}]}]},{"nodeType":"list-item","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"ML classification becomes less effective","marks":[],"data":{}}]}]}]},{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"ReportPortal delivers real value only when defect types are used consistently.","marks":[],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"Common issue:","marks":[{"type":"bold"}],"data":{}},{"nodeType":"text","value":"\nEverything is marked as \"To Investigate\", and nothing gets properly categorized.","marks":[],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"How to avoid it:","marks":[{"type":"bold"}],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"unordered-list","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"list-item","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"Define a clear set of defect types and keep them up to date with your needs","marks":[],"data":{}}]}]},{"nodeType":"list-item","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"Analyze each failure and explicitly assign the correct defect type – this creates training data for the ","marks":[],"data":{}},{"nodeType":"hyperlink","data":{"uri":"https://reportportal.io/docs/analysis/AutoAnalysisOfLaunches"},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"Analyzer","marks":[{"type":"underline"}],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"text","value":", so similar issues can be automatically recognized and categorized correctly in future runs","marks":[],"data":{}}]}]}]},{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"3. Blind trust in ML classification","marks":[{"type":"bold"}],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"ReportPortal’s ML features are powerful, but they are not a replacement for process. Some teams either ignore ML entirely or trust it without verification.","marks":[],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"Problems occur when:","marks":[],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"unordered-list","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"list-item","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"training data is inconsistent","marks":[],"data":{}}]}]},{"nodeType":"list-item","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"predictions are not reviewed","marks":[],"data":{}}]}]},{"nodeType":"list-item","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"test logs are unstable","marks":[],"data":{}}]}]}]},{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"How to avoid it:","marks":[{"type":"bold"}],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"unordered-list","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"list-item","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"Regularly confirm or correct ML predictions. ","marks":[],"data":{}}]}]},{"nodeType":"list-item","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"Ensure consistent logging for similar failures. ","marks":[],"data":{}}]}]},{"nodeType":"list-item","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"Treat ML as an assistant, not a decision-maker","marks":[],"data":{}}]}]}]},{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"4. Mixing incompatible test runs in one analysis","marks":[{"type":"bold"}],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"Another common mistake is analyzing tests with different purposes together:","marks":[],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"unordered-list","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"list-item","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"UI and API tests ","marks":[],"data":{}}]}]},{"nodeType":"list-item","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"smoke and regression suites","marks":[],"data":{}}]}]}]},{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"This makes metrics misleading. A failure rate acceptable for nightly runs may be unacceptable for smoke tests.","marks":[],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"How to avoid it:","marks":[{"type":"bold"}],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"unordered-list","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"list-item","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"Separate launches by test purpose. ","marks":[],"data":{}}]}]},{"nodeType":"list-item","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"Use ","marks":[],"data":{}},{"nodeType":"hyperlink","data":{"uri":"https://reportportal.io/docs/work-with-reports/LaunchesTestItemsAttributes"},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"attributes","marks":[{"type":"underline"}],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"text","value":" or separate projects.","marks":[],"data":{}}]}]}]},{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"5. Underestimating the importance of log quality","marks":[{"type":"bold"}],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"ReportPortal cannot compensate for poor logging. If logs are inconsistent or lack context, analysis becomes manual and slow. Typical issues:","marks":[],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"unordered-list","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"list-item","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"the same error reported differently","marks":[],"data":{}}]}]},{"nodeType":"list-item","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"missing steps or environment details","marks":[],"data":{}}]}]},{"nodeType":"list-item","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"no clear failure reason","marks":[],"data":{}}]}]}]},{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"How to avoid it:","marks":[{"type":"bold"}],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"unordered-list","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"list-item","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"Standardize test logging.","marks":[],"data":{}}]}]},{"nodeType":"list-item","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"Log causes, not just symptoms.","marks":[],"data":{}}]}]},{"nodeType":"list-item","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"Ensure similar failures produce similar messages.","marks":[],"data":{}}]}]}]},{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"6. No clear ownership of test result analysis","marks":[{"type":"bold"}],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"A critical organizational mistake is having \"shared ownership\" of ReportPortal – meaning no ownership at all. When no one is responsible:","marks":[],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"unordered-list","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"list-item","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"dashboards degrade over time","marks":[],"data":{}}]}]},{"nodeType":"list-item","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"defect classification becomes inconsistent","marks":[],"data":{}}]}]},{"nodeType":"list-item","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"insights are lost","marks":[],"data":{}}]}]}]},{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"How to avoid it:","marks":[{"type":"bold"}],"data":{}}]},{"nodeType":"unordered-list","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"list-item","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"Assign a clear owner (for example, QA Lead).","marks":[],"data":{}}]}]},{"nodeType":"list-item","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"Continuously improve dashboards and metrics.","marks":[],"data":{}}]}]},{"nodeType":"list-item","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"Treat ReportPortal as a product, not just a log repository.","marks":[],"data":{}}]}]}]},{"nodeType":"paragraph","data":{},"content":[{"nodeType":"text","value":"ReportPortal is a decision-support tool, not just a reporting system. Most mistakes teams make are process-related, not technical. Teams that define clear analysis goals, invest in defect classification and logging quality, and assign ownership, turn ReportPortal into a powerful driver of quality and efficiency rather than a source of noisy data.","marks":[],"data":{}}]}]}

title.title: Common mistakes when analyzing test results with ReportPortal

leadParagraph.leadParagraph: ReportPortal is widely used for analyzing automated test results, aggregating logs, classifying failures with ML, and tracking quality trends over time. When used correctly, it significantly improves feedback speed and decision-making.
